I reviewed this 59 year old man with symptomatic structural epilepsy in clinic today.
He gets complex partial seizures (deja-vu and orofacial automatsisms) as well as secondary generalised seizures.
He is taking levetiracetam 1500mg bd as well as lamotrigine 200mg bd and clobazam for seizure clusters (he takes this infrequently).
Diagnosis: generalised tonic chronic seizures, absences and myoclonic jerks since teenage years,?JME.
Diagnosis: generalised tonic chronic seizures, absences and myoclonic jerks since teenage years,?JME.
Medication: lamotrigine 150 milligrams twice a day
Keppra 1000 milligrams twice a day
Diagnosis: juvenile myoclonic epilepsy
I think that the jerks are myoclonic seizures
I think that the levetiracetam she is taking could well be contributing to this
Increase the carbamazepine to 600mg twice a day.
Diagnosis:            Focal epilepsy
I think that this is focal onset epilepsy.
The smaller episodes are focal seizures with altered awareness
the episodes during sleep are focal to bilateral convulsive seizures

Diagnosis Focal epilepsy, probable occipital lobe onset
Impression it does seem that this lady has a drug refractory focal (occipital lobe) epilepsy.
Her normal seizures consist of coloured blobbs in the right side of her version.
She might stare and be unresponsive later for a few minutes.
About once a month she will have a secondary generalised seizure which start like her normal seizure but then will progress to involve loss of consciousness with limb shaking.

Medication:
Zonismaide 100mg bd
Brivitiracetam 50mg bd
